claim: Agent skills are folders of instructions, scripts, and resources that agents use to perform tasks with core skill.md file serving as SOP for process execution
actionable: Structure agent skills with skill.md as process instruction and separate reference files for context, examples, and tools
source_title: How to build Claude Skills Better than 99% of People

claim: Progressive disclosure loads only skill metadata into memory initially, full skill.md on trigger, and reference files on-demand to enable thousands of skills per agent
actionable: Implement progressive context loading when building scalable skill systems to manage context window limitations
source_title: How to build Claude Skills Better than 99% of People

claim: Skills enable self-improvement when instructed to automatically update rules and save approved outputs as good examples for future iterations
actionable: Include self-improvement instructions in skill.md to automatically capture approved outputs and refine rules based on usage patterns
source_title: How to build Claude Skills Better than 99% of People

claim: Planning and context engineering (defining process steps, knowledge sources, human-in-loop points) has more impact on skill quality than the initial prompt
actionable: Spend time designing ideal step-by-step process and gathering context files before prompting to build skills
source_title: How to build Claude Skills Better than 99% of People

claim: Multiple output variations with human-in-loop selection (not single outputs) improves skill performance and user adoption more than any other optimization
actionable: Instruct skills to always generate multiple variations (3-5) and ask users to choose rather than presenting single outputs
source_title: How to build Claude Skills Better than 99% of People

claim: Different skill modifications require different approaches: incorrect process logic → modify skill.md; missing context → add reference file; tool usage errors → create MCP documentation
actionable: Use systematic approach to skill improvements: logic issues go to skill.md, information issues to reference files, tool issues to MCP docs
source_title: How to build Claude Skills Better than 99% of People

claim: Skills sit between deterministic automation (workflows) and pure chat - they provide human-in-loop context-dependent judgment at scale without hard-coding business logic
actionable: Use skills for workflows requiring judgment and context-dependency; use deterministic automation for fully clear-cut processes
source_title: How to build Claude Skills Better than 99% of People

claim: Good output examples have more impact on skill performance than prompt engineering alone; example-driven development is key to skill quality
actionable: Always prepare high-quality output examples and include them as reference files before building skills
source_title: How to build Claude Skills Better than 99% of People
